title: requests — model observers
description: This module owns NO models and therefore contributes NO Eloquent observers. State transitions on the underlying `approval_instances` table are observed by workflow/approvals; state transitions on the resulting `access_grants` table are observed by access/grants. This module reacts to those observers' EVENTS via `listeners.json`, not by attaching observers directly.
rationale: Attaching an observer to another module's model would be a boundary violation — downstream ordering surprises + double-write semantics + guessing-game on which observer fires first.
The observer contract is `owner-writes, world-reads-via-events`. This module reads via events + never violates the write path.

If a future feature genuinely needs pre/post-save hooks on approval_instance rows created by this module, the correct move is to add a `#[AsApprovableAction]` metadata hook to workflow/approvals — not to hijack the observer contract.
notes: Distinct from `listeners.json` (this file): observers fire on Eloquent lifecycle events (creating/created/updating/updated/saving/saved/deleting/deleted/restoring/restored) inside the same transaction. Listeners fire on domain events broadcast on the event bus, after commit. This module uses the latter throughout.
